rationale: In the citing paper, Gneezy, Gneezy, and Lauga (2014) appears in an introductory grouped citation supporting a general claim that consumers use imperfect marketplace cues when judging quality. The paper does not single out that study's reference-dependent price-quality mechanism, does not build hypotheses from it, and does not reuse its design or findings in the core theory tests. Its role is broad background context rather than a distinct functional input to the new framework.
